title: crepis crocea subsp. crocea
intro: Crepis crocea subsp. crocea, also known as the yellow hawk's-beard or common hawk's-beard, is a biennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Crepis crocea subsp. crocea include its ability to reduce inflammation and pain, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions and infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
